title: Health, safety and wellbeing - Ministry of Education
content:
## Health, safety and wellbeing
Find out what education professionals need to do to keep students healthy and safe in schools and kura.
- Emergency preparedness 5
- Te tiaki tamariki ki rō kura Child protection in schools There are a number of legal child protection requirements your school must follow.
There are a number of legal child protection requirements your school must follow.
- Ngā aratohu kaupapahere mō ngā pū Firearms policy guidelines There are strict conditions for allowing firearms in schools. We have guidelines to help boards develop firearms policies.
There are strict conditions for allowing firearms in schools. We have guidelines to help boards develop firearms policies.
- Ngā paearu whakaora ohotata ki rō kura First aid requirements in schools Schools must follow minimum standards for first aid in the workplace set by WorkSafe.
Schools must follow minimum standards for first aid in the workplace set by WorkSafe.
- Ngā kupu arataki mō te ako ki waho i te rūma ako Guidelines for education outside the classroom There are clear requirements for school boards and teachers to plan and safely deliver education outside the classroom (EOTC).
There are clear requirements for school boards and teachers to plan and safely deliver education outside the classroom (EOTC).
- Te whakahaere i te hauora me te haumaru o te whakamahi pūrere matihiko a ngā kaimahi me ngā ākonga Health and safety management of digital device use by staff and students School and kura boards are legally responsible for managing the health and safety of staff and students when using digital devices.
School and kura boards are legally responsible for managing the health and safety of staff and students when using digital devices.
- Ngā haepapa haumaru a ngā kura Health and safety responsibilities for schools School boards are legally responsible for the health and safety of everyone working at, attending and visiting a school.
School boards are legally responsible for the health and safety of everyone working at, attending and visiting a school.
- Ngā aratohu mō ngā mate poke kei rō kura Managing infectious disease cases in schools State and state-integrated schools and kura can tell a student not to attend if they suspect that student has an infectious disease.
State and state-integrated schools and kura can tell a student not to attend if they suspect that student has an infectious disease.
- Ngā tātarihanga Pirihimana mā ngā kura Police vets for schools and kura All staff and contractors who have unsupervised access to children at a school must be Police vetted
All staff and contractors who have unsupervised access to children at a school must be Police vetted
- Te whakarite mō te tūpono ohotata, mō te tūpono pāmamaetanga rānei ki rō kura Prepare for an emergency or traumatic incident All schools must have an emergency management plan in place. This is the responsibility of the school board.
All schools must have an emergency management plan in place. This is the responsibility of the school board.
- Te whakatairanga i ngā kai hauora ki rō kura Promoting healthy food and nutrition in schools School boards must promote healthy food and nutrition to students under the Education (School Boards) Amendment Regulations 2022.
School boards must promote healthy food and nutrition to students under the Education (School Boards) Amendment Regulations 2022.
- Te whakapuaki i ngā whakapae tūkino, whakahapa rānei i ngā tamariki me ngā rangatahi nā te pakeke Report suspected abuse or neglect of children and young people by adults If you suspect abuse or neglect of a child or young person by an adult, you should report it. Find out who you can contact.
If you suspect abuse or neglect of a child or young person by an adult, you should report it. Find out who you can contact.
- Te urupare ki ngā tūpono ohotata, ki ngā tūpono pāmamaetanga rānei ki rō kura Respond at school to an emergency or traumatic incident Responsibilities and care for teachers, students, parents, whānau and your community during and after an emergency or traumatic incident.
Responsibilities and care for teachers, students, parents, whānau and your community during and after an emergency or traumatic incident.
